raw.title: Andrew Judah
raw.description:
Since the summer of 2022, Tuesday nights at micro + bar and bites are home to live music. Our curated schedule is comprised of talented musicians, performing in an intimate, listening-style environment. And of course, the music is designed to be enjoyed with friends new and old over creative cocktails and tasty tapas.

Over 100+ nights of music have been held at our cozy bar, featuring a variety of Okanagan talent representing genres such as singer-songwriter, soul, blues, latin folclórica, and eastern-European jazz.

Given that our seating is limited (and we do not take reservations), we recommend you come early to secure your spot at the bar and enjoy an unforgettable night of live music, great cocktails, and a few bites from our tapas menu.

raw.description:
Free Program - Registration Required to secure your spot: https://www.rdco.intellionline.ca/WEBSITE/public/category/browse/BATNGHT

Using special audio detectors, we’ll tune into bat echolocation calls and uncover the secret lives of bats. On this evening nature walk, we’ll be watching for bats in action and learning about their nocturnal habits, ecological importance, and conservation needs.  

For more information on other programs offered by the RDCO and registration details, please visit our website: https://www.rdco.intellionline.ca/WEBSITE/public.

raw.title: Blossom & Bites: A Spring Lunch for Two
raw.description:
A Springtime Meadery lunch for TWO! Enjoy the Spring Sunshine on our farm! Start with a Local, Charcuterie Bento Lunch in our Picnic Area or our picturesque outdoor Bistro and a guided wine tasting or flight of our honey wines. Take our self-guided Bee Tour, take the Honeybee Challenge, play lawn games & enjoy farm fun.  Finish your experience with a scrumptious local Bernard Callebaut Chocolate Brownie with homemade honey ice cream for two! 

Includes:

Bento Local Charcuterie for two

Local meats & cheeses, house-made pickles, fresh fruit, house-made organic crostinis, homemade honey jams & accoutrements

2 Guided Wine Tastings featuring five of our award-winning honey wines  (local mocktails for non-drinkers or under 19!)

Bernard Callebaut Chocolate Brownie dessert with homemade honey ice cream to share

Self-Guided Bee Tour

Lawn games and picnic area

Take the Honeybee Challenge

Entry into our Spring Draw

*Please select a time slot between 11:30 am and 3:30 pm. Tickets required. We look forward to seeing you at the winery!

raw.description:
Enjoy Dynamic Duo once a month on Tuesdays! Curtis Kieres & Dennis Lyne are two accomplished musicians who joined forces to provide high-quality entertainment for all to enjoy.

Curtis is a professional saxophonist, composer and multi-instrumentalist who brings a sultry and soothing sound that is characteristic of the saxophone.

Dennis is a versatile keyboardist and accomplished vocalist who has woven a rich tapestry of musical experiences throughout his career. Together, they perform the blues & rock classics in a variety of venues in BC.

events.title: Floor Opener
events.description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbu

raw.title: Floor Opener
raw.description: Lawrence Paul Yuxweluptun is one of Canada’s most outspoken and influential contemporary artists, confronting colonial suppression, environmental degradation, and the ongoing struggles for Indigenous sovereignty. Of Cowichan (Hul’q’umi’num Coast Salish) and Okanagan (Syilx) descent, his forty-year practice brings together traditional iconography, fearless critique, and biting satire to challenge and unsettle. Floor Opener presents a selection of works that are at once polemical and playful, imbued with a spiritual resonance that speaks urgently to the political and social realities of our time. This nationally significant exhibition is organized and circulated by the Kitchener-Waterloo Art Gallery, with artwork sourced from private collections in British Columbia.

events.title: Free Public Skating
events.description: Come join us for Public Skating at Winfield Arena. A fun and welcoming session for skaters of all ages and abilities! This session is open to everyone and offers a relaxed, recreational atmosphere. All participants are encouraged to skate responsibly and be mindful of others on the ice. Helmets are strongly recommended for all skaters and are mandatory for children. Rentals are not available.

events.title: From Then Until Now: A Michael Adamson Painting Survey
events.description: From Then Until Now: A Michael Adamson Painting Survey brings together works from the 1990s to 2026 and shows how Adamson’s painting has evolved over more than three decades.Painting has often been declared “dead,” especially with the rise of conceptual art and now AI-generated images, but Adamson’s work is proof that painting is still very much alive. Digital tools can quickly produce polished images, but paintings focus on touch, material, and human intuition. hey are slower, more uncertain, a

raw.recurrence: Recurring weekly on Sunday, Monday, Tuesday, Wednesday, Thursday, Friday, Saturday
raw.title: From Then Until Now: A Michael Adamson Painting Survey
raw.description:
From Then Until Now: A Michael Adamson Painting Survey brings together works from the 1990s to 2026 and shows how Adamson’s painting has evolved over more than three decades.

Painting has often been declared “dead,” especially with the rise of conceptual art and now AI-generated images, but Adamson’s work is proof that painting is still very much alive. Digital tools can quickly produce polished images, but paintings focus on touch, material, and human intuition. hey are slower, more uncertain, and more personal.

Adamson trained at a time in the 1990s when photography and digital media were becoming dominant, but rather than turning away from painting, he absorbed those influences into it with his work that emphasizes what makes painting distinct, its physical presence and emotional weight.

This exhibition doesn’t present a strict timeline, but more so a consistent approach to seeing and making. Adamson continues to explore perception and how images come into being, and in a fast-moving, image-saturated world, his paintings offer a slower experience.

We ask viewers to pause, look carefully, and spend time with what they see.

-A Note from Michael Adamson and Louis Elmer

raw.title: Memorial Cup Wine & Water Experiences & Private Lake Cruises
raw.description:
Visiting Kelowna during the Memorial Cup? Experience the Okanagan beyond the arena with a private lake cruise, sunset cruise, or curated Wine & Water experience with Lake Life Pontoon Cruises.

Enjoy scenic cruising on Okanagan Lake aboard a private luxury pontoon with a professional captain, or explore the Central Okanagan with private winery transportation in our newly branded premium touring van.

Experiences are perfect for hockey fans, families, corporate groups, and visitors looking to relax and experience the best of Kelowna during Memorial Cup week.

Private experiences available throughout the event week:
• Scenic lake cruises
• Sunset cruises
• Wine & Water experiences
• Private winery transportation
• Custom group experiences

Advance booking recommended.

raw.title: Stillness & Sound Yin Yoga + Crystal Sound Bowl in the Himalayan Salt Cave!
raw.description:
Unwind in the soft glow of the Himalayan Salt Cave as you’re guided through an evening of deep rest, gentle movement, and healing sound.

Stillness & Sound is a calming Yin Yoga practice paired with the soothing vibrations of a single crystal sound bowl and delicate chimes — creating a peaceful and grounding experience designed to melt away stress and restore balance.

Held in the tranquil environment of the salt cave, this practice invites you to slow down, breathe deeply, and reconnect with yourself on every level — physical, emotional, and energetic.

What to Expect:

A slow, supportive Yin Yoga sequence with long-held postures

Gentle sound throughout using one crystal sound bowl and chimes, enhancing the meditative state

A deeply restorative atmosphere lit by the warm glow of salt walls

Breath awareness and moments of quiet reflection

A space to release tension and reset your nervous system

🧂 Why the Salt Cave?

Practicing in the Himalayan Salt Cave offers more than serenity — it provides the natural benefits of dry salt therapy (halotherapy). A halo-generator disperses fine, pharmaceutical-grade salt particles into the air, helping to:

Support respiratory health and lung function

Reduce inflammation and ease muscular tension

Promote detoxification and skin clarity

Enhance relaxation, calm, and clarity

When combined with yoga and sound, the healing effects of the salt cave are amplified — creating a full-body experience of renewal and restoration.
